title: Melanie Twitt

infobox.value: |  | | --- | --- | |  | | | Women's [field hockey](/wiki/Field_hockey "Field hockey") | | | | Representing  [Australia](/wiki/Australia "Australia") | | | | [Commonwealth Games](/wiki/Hockey_at_the_Commonwealth_Games "Hockey at the Commonwealth Games") | | | | Gold medal – first place | [2006 Melbourne](/wiki/2006_Commonwealth_Games "2006 Commonwealth Games") | [Team](/wiki/Hockey_at_the_2006_Commonwealth_Games_%E2%80%93_Women%27s_tournament "Hockey at the 2006 Commonwealth Games – Women's tournament") | |

(born 7 October 1977) is an Australian [field hockey](/wiki/Field_hockey "Field hockey") player who competed in the [2004 Summer Olympics](/wiki/2004_Summer_Olympics "2004 Summer Olympics") and in the [2008 Summer Olympics](/wiki/2008_Summer_Olympics "2008 Summer Olympics").[[1]](#cite_note-sref-1)[[2]](#cite_note-2)

Evans, Hilary; Gjerde, Arild; Heijmans, Jeroen; [Mallon, Bill](/wiki/Bill_Mallon "Bill Mallon"); et al. ["Melanie Twitt"](https://web.archive.org/web/20200418083100/https://www.sports-reference.com/olympics/athletes/tw/melanie-twitt-wells-1.html). *Olympics at Sports-Reference.com*. [Sports Reference LLC](/wiki/Sports_Reference "Sports Reference"). Archived from [the original](https://www.sports-reference.com/olympics/athletes/tw/melanie-twitt-wells-1.html) on 18 April 2020. Retrieved 25 May 2012.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Australian field hockey player

Melanie Twitt

| Medal record  |  |  | | --- | --- | |  | | | Women's [field hockey](/wiki/Field_hockey "Field hockey") | | | | Representing  [Australia](/wiki/Australia "Australia") | | | | [Commonwealth Games](/wiki/Hockey_at_the_Commonwealth_Games "Hockey at the Commonwealth Games") | | | | Gold medal – first place | [2006 Melbourne](/wiki/2006_Commonwealth_Games "2006 Commonwealth Games") | [Team](/wiki/Hockey_at_the_2006_Commonwealth_Games_%E2%80%93_Women%27s_tournament "Hockey at the 2006 Commonwealth Games – Women's tournament") | | |

**Melanie Twitt** (born 7 October 1977) is an Australian [field hockey](/wiki/Field_hockey "Field hockey") player who competed in the [2004 Summer Olympics](/wiki/2004_Summer_Olympics "2004 Summer Olympics") and in the [2008 Summer Olympics](/wiki/2008_Summer_Olympics "2008 Summer Olympics").[[1]](#cite_note-sref-1)[[2]](#cite_note-2)

["Melanie Twitt"](https://web.archive.org/web/20200418083100/https://www.sports-reference.com/olympics/athletes/tw/melanie-twitt-wells-1.html). *Olympics at Sports-Reference.com*. [Sports Reference LLC](/wiki/Sports_Reference "Sports Reference"). Archived from [the original](https://www.sports-reference.com/olympics/athletes/tw/melanie-twitt-wells-1.html) on 18 April 2020. Retrieved 25 May 2012.
2. **[^](#cite_ref-2)** ["Melanie Wells"](https://www.olympics.com.au/olympians/melanie-wells/). *Australian Olympic Committee*. Retrieved 5 June 2021.
